steps.title: Set up the density formula
steps.content: We know that Density = Mass / Volume, so we can rearrange to find Mass = Density × Volume. The density is 926 kg/m^3, and we need to find the volume of a cube with edge length 0.70 meters.

steps.title: Calculate the cube's volume
steps.content:
For a cube with edge length $s$, the volume is $s^3$. With $s = 0.70$ meters:
$$V = (0.70)^3 = 0.70 \cdot 0.70 \cdot 0.70 = 0.343 \text{ m}^3$$

steps.title: Multiply density by volume to find mass
steps.content:
Now we apply the formula:
$$\text{Mass} = 926 \cdot 0.343 = 317.618 \text{ kg}$$
Rounding to the nearest whole number gives 318 kg. That matches Choice A — $318$.
